items.title: Australian government arguing 'very forcefully' against Trump's new 12.5pc tariffs

children.content: A new levy on exports from Australia into the US has taken effect, superseding the 10 per cent tariff that was expiring. Dozens of other countries have also been hit, but Australia's tariff is among the highest.

articles.title: Why did Trump slap a 12.5pc tariff on Australian imports?

children.content: The tariffs are supposed to combat forced labour, but one expert says the US president was just looking for another legal way to tax foreign imports.

image.altText: A US flag flies near a ship at a shipping port

gallery.altText: A man in a blue suit with white hair speaks to someone out of camera with a white and red striped flag behind him

thumbnail.altText: A man in a blue suit with white hair speaks to someone out of camera with a white and red striped flag behind him

avatar.alt: Brad Ryan is pictured in front of a news studio background, which shows a bridge in a city.

items.title: Angus Taylor dismisses Trump's 'forced labour' tariff justification

children.content: Opposition Leader Angus Taylor says he's "dead against" the Trump administration's tariffs on Australian goods set to come into force later today, dismissing the notion it has anything to do with modern slavery laws. Follow live.

image.altText: Opposition leader Angus Taylor wearing a suit and speaking at a press conference.

items.title: Four Corners withdraws episodes by sacked reporter

children.content: The ABC has withdrawn the Four Corners programs Meth Highway and Cocaine Nation by sacked reporter Mahmood Fazal due to concerns about accuracy.

image.altText: A headshot of Mahmood Fazal, bearded and in a cap.

items.title: Radical groups have a shared hatred of Jews, royal commission hears

children.content: The chief executive of a foundation combating antisemitism says extremist groups from across the political spectrum are posing an unprecedented threat to Australia's Jewish community.

image.altText: Tahli Blicblau

items.title: TGA issues warning over rare eye disorder linked to Ozempic-like drugs

children.content: The Therapeutic Goods Administration has issued a safety warning for a rare, but serious risk of vision impairment from taking GLP-1-based drugs such as Ozempic and Wegovy.

image.altText: Two boxes of medication side by side, labelled Ozempic and Wegovy

items.title: Drivers urged not to panic buy as fuel price hikes nowhere near record high

children.content: Australians are being urged by a peak motorist association not to panic buy fuel amid a flare-up of tensions in the Middle East, saying prices are nowhere near April's record high.

articles.title: Track the latest petrol and diesel prices around Australia

children.content: See what's happening with petrol and diesel prices near you, updated constantly.

articles.title: Petrol supply could 'tighten' as oil jumps over $US100 a barrel

children.content: The entry of the Houthi rebels into the war between the US and Iran has threatened another vital shipping route through the Middle East.

image.altText: Cars queue at a petrol station.

gallery.altText: BP petrol station showing bowsers and prices 182.9 cents a litre for unleaded, deisel 220.9

thumbnail.altText: BP petrol station showing bowsers and prices 182.9 cents a litre for unleaded, deisel 220.9

items.title: RBA rate hike calls grow as oil surges

children.content: Markets and some commentators are now expecting an August RBA interest rate hike following a surge in oil prices above $US100 a barrel and persistent inflation. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the live markets blog.

image.altText: RBA governor Michele Bullock at press conference on June 18, 2024

items.title: AI has changed what criminals can do with your stolen Origin data

children.content: The theft of names, addresses and phone numbers may seem less serious than stolen bank details, but cyber experts say AI is changing what criminals can do with that information.

image.altText: Origin energy img

items.title: Major report finds flaws in Australia's spy laws

children.content: A major review warns parts of Australia's national security laws could undermine democracy and press freedom.

image.altText: A man in a dark blue suit and a patterned blue tie speaks at a microphone at a lectern.

items.title: Socceroos star allegedly tests positive for cocaine after speeding

children.content: Police say they stopped the 22-year-old in a BMW Coupe about 1am today, allegedly travelling 109 kilometres per hour on the Anzac Bridge.

image.altText: A man rips at his jersey while celebrating on the soccer field.

items.title: The Commonwealth Games have never opened like this, but maybe they should

children.content: The 2026 Commonwealth Games are like no other that came before them and the opening ceremony was similarly unique in a way that will make it hard to go back.

articles.title: King Charles opens Glasgow 2026 during ceremony buoyed by Scottish pride

children.content: King Charles III officially opens the Commonwealth Games as many of the 3,000 athletes parade in front of 14,000 spectators.

image.altText: GettyImages-2286694433

articles.title: Glasgow was asleep and the Games weren't open as Australia got its first win

children.content: Australia's first victory of the Commonwealth Games comes in a surprise extra session as the bowlers start before the opening ceremony.

image.altText: Kelsey Cottrell high fives Australian women's pairs bowls teammate Dawn Hayman at the Glasgow 2026 Commonwealth Games.

articles.title: Your guide to every day of the Comm Games in Australian time

children.content: With 10 sports crammed into 10 full days of action in Glasgow, it may be hard to keep track of what's on and when at the Commonwealth Games. So, here's the full schedule of every day of the 2026 Glasgow Commonwealth Games with all times and days in AEST.

image.altText: A basketballer spins a ball on his finger, a boxer poses, a weightlifter holds up a barbell, and a wheelchair basketballer.

items.title: Chalmers praises Rinehart for bonuses amid warning to not thank billionaire

children.content: Swimming star Kyle Chalmers praises billionaire Gina Rinehart over bonuses for Commonwealth Games medals, as a report suggests athletes have been told not to thank the mining magnate.

image.altText: A man covered in tattoos sits poolside ahead of training.

items.title: Sam Kerr says A-League must find way to bring Matildas back, pay more

children.content: Matildas captain Sam Kerr is already thriving in the next chapter of her career with Gotham FC in the NWSL, but she wants the A-League Women to get back to its previous glory days, with more investment in the semi-professional league.

image.altText: Gotham FC player Sam Kerr celebrates with a smile and arms outstretched after her team scores

items.title: Adolescence creator tackles teen rehab very differently in The Good Boy

children.content: Emmy Award-winning Adolescence co-creator Stephen Graham tackles teen delinquency in a very different way in The Good Boy.

items.title: Budjerah's debut album Gentleman has been a long time coming

children.content: Two-time ARIA Award-winning music artist Budjerah has just released his long-awaited debut album and announced an official tour.

items.title: Friday news quiz: World Cup winners, rock legends and Barnaby Joyce's dog

children.content: We have 10 questions to test your knowledge of the week in … SIT DOWN! Sorry, it's the ABC news quiz.

items.title: Security concerns in US over Chinese ships that visit Australia

children.content: Two Chinese polar research ships that make regular port visits in Australia have come under increased scrutiny by US authorities targeting "any detected malign activity".

thumbnail.altText: Two red and white ships break through an icy body of water.

items.title: Automakers in shock as China takes over

children.content: In June, a bulk carrier made an unprecedented delivery of EVs to Australia, sounding an ominous warning to the world's automakers.

image.altText: A large red and white ship sits in a dock as a white car is driven off its ramp

items.title: Zelenskyy just scored an 'own goal' and it cost him big time

children.content: The Ukrainian president's decision to fire his popular, tech-savvy 35-year-old Defence Minister Mykhailo Fedorov last week baffled many. Analysts say what happens next could be pivotal to the war.

image.altText: A man looks on with a serious expression. His face is seen from side on.

items.title: The text warnings about a looming Liberal Party corruption scandal

children.content: Nineteen months before making the parliamentary speech that sparked a corruption inquiry into NSW Liberal figures and their relationship with a fugitive property developer, parliamentary secretary Ray Williams sent an urgent text message to his boss, premier Gladys Berejiklian.

image.altText: Gladys Berejiklian looking worried.

items.title: Norway set to file complaint over Trump's push to overturn red card

children.content: Norway's soccer federation says football is on a "slippery slope" after FIFA overturned a World Cup red card under pressure from US president Donald Trump.

image.altText: Gianni Infantino holds the World Cup trophy with Donald Trump

items.title: Conspiracy theories about AusAlert test spreading online

children.content: From wrapping your phone in foil to secret phones, ABC NEWS Verify takes a look into some of these false claims and real concerns about AusAlert to try to answer a few questions.

items.title: Man linked to Isla Bell death fails to have case thrown out

children.content: Marat Ganiev was seeking a permanent stay on his case, with his lawyer arguing the multiple changes to the prosecution case were an abuse of process.

image.altText: Photo of a young woman with red hair and nose piercings

items.title: Worker dead, another injured as truck rolls over vehicle at Queensland mine

children.content: One man is dead and another has been seriously injured after their vehicle was struck by a giant mining truck at a Central Queensland coal site.

image.altText: A close up image of a plaque honouring fallen miners at the Moranbah memorial.

items.title: Matt Wright representing himself in lawsuit brought by co-star's widow

children.content: A Federal Court case against celebrity crocodile wrangler Matt Wright could take another year before making it to trial. The lawsuit has been brought by Danielle Wilson, the widow of Mr Wright's TV co-star Chris "Willow" Wilson, who died in a 2022 chopper crash.

image.altText: A close-up photo of a man with blue eyes, wearing a light-coloured shirt as he walks out of court.

items.title: Modra's medical records allegedly inappropriately accessed by SA Health staff

children.content: Three SA Health staff are being investigated for alleged inappropriate access to the private medical records of AFL star Tony Modra, while a further five are being "assessed" to determine whether their access to the records was appropriate.

image.altText: A man wearing a black shirt smiles.

items.title: Stage winner almost hits overzealous fan in gruelling Tour de France climb

children.content: Richard Carapaz almost hits a fan distracted by a TV camera as the Ecuadorian claims the 18th stage of the Tour de France.

gallery.altText: A fan gets in the way of cyclists.

thumbnail.altText: A fan gets in the way of cyclists.

items.title: White House will not fight Tate brothers extradition to UK

children.content: US President Donald Trump will not stop the extradition of influencer Andrew Tate and his brother Tristan to Britain on sex crime charges, the White House has said.

image.altText: two men standing near each other

items.title: Interest rates don't just hit households. Small business is feeling the pinch

children.content: Small-to-medium sized businesses have had a challenging year, with increased costs and regulatory reforms. Insolvency rates are beginning to rise and more owners are shopping around for credit.

image.altText: Worker with mask on welding

items.title: After Brat, Charli xcx asks a bigger question about legacy

children.content: On her new album Music, Fashion, Film, British artist Charli xcx discusses the enduring legacy of art and artists, and how creative figures are remembered long after their moment has passed.

items.title: First suspected positive case of H5 bird flu detected in Queensland

children.content: It's the first wild, migratory seabird in Queensland to return a suspect positive case for H5 bird flu.

image.altText: Beach in front Tangalooma Resort on Moreton Island off Brisbane on May 1, 2018.

items.title: New victim identification details released amid Sydney childcare probe

children.content: Police are still trying to identify a child from a Putney childcare centre in Sydney's north who was found pictured on electronic devices seized from Hamish Tait as part of the investigation.

image.altText: Cloise up of man grinning in a green shirt, plant behind him

items.title: When Dewi gave up her baby, she immediately regretted the decision

children.content: Police in Indonesia are more regularly busting baby trafficking and illegal adoption rings, with officials worried poverty may be driving the practice.

image.altText: Documents related to child protection in Indonesia.

items.title: Thousands living 'as Australians in Australia' without a nationality

children.content: As a Rohingya person from Myanmar, Asma was denied an official nationality. Migration experts say statelessness, an issue faced by over 8,000 people in Australia, remains poorly understood.

image.altText: Asma, wearing a brown headscarf and glasses, stares into the distance in front of a glass window

items.title: First nursery for babies of Ebola victims opens in Congo

children.content: The first UNICEF-supported Ebola nursery opens in the Democratic Republic of Congo, supporting young children whose parents have contracted the disease while the number of cases intensifies.

Wivine Kabla with an infant at the nursery in DRC. She wears protective gear on her head and a scrubs.
